channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Entrevista con Tucker Carlson
item.description: El destacado comentarista conservador se distanció del gobierno de Trump por la guerra en Irán. ¿Durará la ruptura?
item.dc:creator: Lulu Garcia-Navarro
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Philip Montgomery for The New York Times

item.title: ¿El secreto para comer menos es amar la comida?
item.description: Se suele pensar que nos deleitamos tanto con la comida que consumimos mucha más cantidad de la que necesitamos. Pero ¿y si el verdadero problema es que no la gozamos lo suficiente?
item.dc:creator: Pete Wells

media:content.media:credit: Ricardo Tomás

item.title: Rusia atrae africanos para combatir en Ucrania
item.description: Cada vez más hombres de todo el continente africano se van a Rusia en pos de una promesa de empleo, solo para terminar siendo obligados a participar en la guerra: “Yo luchaba por Rusia. Tenía un uniforme ruso. Pero no era mi lucha”.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Mpoke Bigg, John Eligon, Zimasa Matiwane and Ed Ram

media:content.media:credit: Ed Ram for The New York Times

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for May 5, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: How Times Reporters Talk to Their Kids About the News
item.description: Four journalists share how they discuss the sensitive topics they cover, such as war and gun violence, with their young ones.
item.dc:creator: Daniel E. Slotnik

item.title: Mingling With Jeff Bezos and Stevie Nicks at the 2026 Met Gala Cocktail Party
item.description: Guests had many interpretations for the “Fashion Is Art” dress code.
item.dc:creator: Sandra E. Garcia and Landon Nordeman

media:content.media:description: Blue Ivy Carter and Beyoncé making their entrance.

item.title: Late Night Laments Losing Spirit Airlines as a Punchline
item.description: The budget carrier announced that “‘all flights have been canceled, and customer service is no longer available,’ which, coincidentally, was also Spirit’s motto,” Stephen Colbert said.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)
Real Time With Bill Maher (TV Program)

item.title: Palabra del día en inglés: ‘superfluous’
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 41 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Word of the Day: superfluous
item.description: This word has appeared in 41 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Oil Prices Slide but Remain Elevated as Middle East Tensions Escalate
item.description: Oil prices fell, giving back some of the gains from the previous day as attacks in the Middle East raised concerns about the war starting again.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Stephane Mahe/Reuters

item.title: U.S. Military Strikes Boat in Caribbean, Killing 2
item.description: The aerial strike raised the death toll to at least 187 in the U.S. campaign against boats the Trump administration accuses of smuggling drugs.
item.dc:creator: John Yoon
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: U.S. Southern Command
media:content.media:description: An image from a video released by U.S. Southern Command showing a lethal kinetic strike on a vessel conducted by Joint Task Force Southern Spear.

item.title: Por qué algunos africanos combaten por Rusia en Ucrania
item.description: La falta de empleo y promesas de trabajo llevan a jóvenes africanos a un conflicto lejano y peligroso.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Mpoke Bigg

media:content.media:credit: Ed Ram para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Familiares de personas reclutadas por Rusia para la guerra de Ucrania protestaron en Nairobi, Kenia, en marzo. La falta de información sobre sus seres queridos desaparecidos impulsó su llamado a los legisladores.

item.title: ¿No puedes dormir? Ojo con los suplementos para el sueño
item.description: Muchas personas utilizan somníferos como el magnesio, la melatonina o el CBD para ayudarles a conciliar el sueño, pero puede no ser la respuesta.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Schupak
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

media:content.media:credit: Tonje Thilesen para The New York Times

item.title: Democrats, Keir Starmer Is a Warning
item.description: Keir Starmer’s collapse was written into the nature of his victory.
item.dc:creator: Samuel Earle

media:content.media:credit: Suzanne Plunkett/Reuters

item.title: Fireworks Factory Explosion in China Kills at Least 21
item.description: Dozens more were injured in the explosion, which appeared to be one of the country’s deadliest in recent years.
item.dc:creator: Chris Buckley

media:content.media:credit: Cnsphoto, via Reuters
media:content.media:description: The site of an explosion at a fireworks factory in Liuyang, China, on Monday. China has improved its record on industrial accidents in recent years, but fireworks factories remain a vulnerability.

item.title: What a Bike Ride in Cape Town Taught Me About Apartheid
item.description: A New York Times reporter joined a group of cyclists on a route meant to break down Cape Town’s lingering racial and economic barriers.
item.dc:creator: John Eligon and Tommy Trenchard

media:content.media:credit: Tommy Trenchard for The New York Times

item.title: Taiwan Could Learn From Ukraine. Informally, Connections Are Growing.
item.description: The governments have no official diplomatic or military ties. But a loose network led by company executives and volunteers is bridging some of that gulf.
item.dc:creator: Maria Varenikova and Amy Chang Chien

media:content.media:credit: Finbarr O’Reilly for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Members of a Ukrainian drone regiment in the Kharkiv region in September. Taiwan is eager to learn from Ukraine’s experience, especially when it comes to drone warfare.

item.title: As Oil Prices Stay High, China Doubles Down on Wind Power
item.description: An industrial policy of subsidies and import restrictions laid the foundations for China to become almost as dominant in wind turbines as in solar panels.
item.dc:creator: Keith Bradsher and Qilai Shen
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Corrections: May 5, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Tuesday, May 5, 2026.

item.title: Our 15 Favorite Looks at the 2026 Met Gala
item.description: Regardless of the theme, these people just looked great.
item.dc:creator: The Styles Desk

media:content.media:credit: Nina Westervelt for The New York Times

item.title: Visible Nipples Were the New Naked Dressing on the 2026 Met Gala Red Carpet
item.description: The look was in keeping with the Met Gala’s accompanying exhibition at the Costume Institute.
item.dc:creator: Alisha Haridasani Gupta

media:content.media:credit: Nina Westervelt for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Kylie Jenner posed in a custom Schiaparelli gown.

item.title: Man Accused of Starting Palisades Fire Admired Luigi Mangione, Prosecutors Say
item.description: The Los Angeles suspect was lonely and angry and felt “enslaved” by rich people, prosecutors say. After being romantically spurned, they say, he went into the mountains and lit a fire.
item.dc:creator: Soumya Karlamangla

media:content.media:credit: Mark Abramson for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Palisades fire killed 12 people and destroyed thousands of homes in the seaside Los Angeles neighborhood of Pacific Palisades in January 2025.

item.title: Connor Storrie and Alysa Liu Are Among the First-Time Met Gala Attendees
item.description: Some of those making their Met debuts also happened to have the evening’s most arresting looks.
item.dc:creator: Yola Mzizi

media:content.media:credit: Nina Westervelt for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Connor Storrie of “Heated Rivalry” at the Met Gala.

item.title: Shootings On the National Mall Are Rare
item.description: The National Mall and its surroundings are one of the safest areas in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Gaya Gupta
item.category: US Federal Takeover of Metropolitan Police Dept (DC)

media:content.media:credit: Kylie Cooper/Reuters
media:content.media:description: The National Mall last month.

item.title: No Reverse-Aging for Bad Bunny at the Met Gala
item.description: The singer, 32, said it took “53 years” to prepare his look.
item.dc:creator: Nathan Taylor Pemberton

media:content.media:credit: Nina Westervelt for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Bad Bunny opted to age himself up, while many other guests worked to look younger.

item.title: Vote for Your Favorite Met Gala Looks
item.description: You’ve seen the fashion from the Met Gala. Now, the Styles desk wants you to rank your favorites.
item.dc:creator: Nina Westervelt

media:content.media:credit: Nina Westervelt for The New York Times

item.title: Jeff Bezos and Mark Zuckerberg Were Notably Missing From the Met Gala Carpet
item.description: No Bezos. No Zuckerberg.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher

media:content.media:credit: Landon Nordeman for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Jeff Bezos saved his socializing for inside the event.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for May 5, 2026
item.description: Max Schlenker’s puzzle will help you ease into the week.
item.dc:creator: Alex Barron

media:content.media:credit: Vincent Alban/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: What info are you storing in your head?

item.title: Sin explicaciones, EE. UU. revocó visas a algunos miembros de La Nación, en Costa Rica
item.description: El gobierno estadounidense revocó las visas de cinco de siete miembros de la junta directiva del principal periódico de investigación en Costa Rica.
item.dc:creator: Emiliano Rodríguez Mega

media:content.media:credit: Mark Schiefelbein/Agence France-Presse, vía Getty Images
media:content.media:description: El secretario de Estado Marco Rubio, a la izquierda, con el presidente de Costa Rica, Rodrigo Chaves, el año pasado

item.title: Trump Tries to Downplay Economic Effects of the Iran War
item.description: At a White House event for Small Business Week, he described the economy as “roaring” and predicted that gas prices would go down soon.
item.dc:creator: Erica L. Green and Zolan Kanno-Youngs
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Allison Robbert for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump speaking to small-business owners before Monday’s event, which the White House described as highlighting “the extraordinary revival of Main Street under his America First agenda.”

item.title: Heidi Klum Turned to Stone for Her Met Gala Look
item.description: The model’s trompe l’oeil outfit would have been equally at home at one of her Halloween parties.
item.dc:creator: Alisha Haridasani Gupta

media:content.media:credit: Nina Westervelt for The New York Times

item.title: Sabrina Carpenter Wears ‘Sabrina’ Film Dress to the Met Gala
item.description: It was perhaps the Met Gala’s most meta moment.
item.dc:creator: Jesse McKinley

media:content.media:credit: Nina Westervelt for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: “Sabrina” on Sabrina.

item.title: Supreme Court Agrees to Fast-Track Louisiana Voting Map Decision
item.description: Louisiana voters who successfully challenged the state’s voting map as an illegal racial gerrymander had asked the justices to quickly return the case to the lower courts, clearing the way for a new map.
item.dc:creator: Abbie VanSickle and Emily Cochrane

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Supreme Court’s landmark opinion weakening the Voting Rights Act has set off a chaotic scramble in Louisiana ahead of a primary for House races.

item.title: Trump Administration Demands Names of 2020 Election Workers in Georgia
item.description: The Justice Department again appears to be using the investigative power of the federal government to rehash debunked claims that Democrats stole the 2020 election.
item.dc:creator: Nick Corasaniti, Richard Fausset and Alan Feuer

media:content.media:credit: Nicole Craine for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: F.B.I. agents raided Fulton County election offices in January, seizing boxes of ballots and other materials.

item.title: Some New York City Democrats Went to the Met Gala
item.description: Mayor Mamdani sat out the fund-raiser. But a number of Democratic city politicians, past and present attended the extravagant affair.
item.dc:creator: Nathan Taylor Pemberton

media:content.media:credit: Nina Westervelt for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Nantasha Williams, the deputy speaker for New York’s City Council, arrives at the Met Gala.

item.title: At the Met Gala, Hands and Arms Appear on the Red Carpet
item.description: Lisa wasn’t the only celebrity to show up at the Met Gala with extra appendages.
item.dc:creator: Gina Cherelus

media:content.media:credit: Nina Westervelt for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Jordan Roth, the Tony Award-winning producer and performer, wore a velvet gown with a figure resembling a conjoined twin peeking out from behind.

item.title: Beyoncé, Blue Ivy and Jay-Z Arrive at the 2026 Met Gala
item.description: The superstar, a co-chair of this year’s gala, electrified onlookers when she arrived with her elder daughter and husband.
item.dc:creator: Melina Delkic

media:content.media:credit: Nina Westervelt for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Beyoncé arrives at the Met Gala.

item.title: Zendaya, Bella Hadid and Other Stars Rumored to Skip the Met Gala
item.description: Some luminaries had scheduling conflicts, but there were some who chose not to attend to make a statement.
item.dc:creator: Alisha Haridasani Gupta

media:content.media:credit: Monica Schipper/Getty Images; Scott A Garfitt/Invision, via Associated Press

item.title: La Gala del Met enfrenta críticas por la participación de Jeff Bezos
item.description: Hay una campaña anti-Bezos en las calles de Nueva York donde han descrito el acto como la “Gala Amazon Prime” o el “Baile de Bezos”.
item.dc:creator: Jesse McKinley and Alisha Haridasani Gupta

media:content.media:credit: Shannon Stapleton/Reuters
media:content.media:description: En las semanas previas al evento, una campaña anti-Bezos se ha visto en las calles y metros de Nueva York. En las redes sociales, los usuarios han descrito el acto como la “Gala Amazon Prime” o el “Baile de Bezos” .

item.title: U.S. Revokes Visas of Board Members at Costa Rica’s Top Watchdog Newspaper
item.description: The move came after the paper critically covered the country’s president, who has cozied up to the United States. Analysts view the visa decision as part of a White House strategy to punish critics of its allies.
item.dc:creator: Emiliano Rodríguez Mega

media:content.media:credit: Mark Schiefelbein/Agence France-Presse, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio, left, with President Rodrigo Chaves of Costa Rica last year.

item.title: Kennedy Starts a Push to Help Americans Quit Antidepressants
item.description: The health secretary has long complained that Americans overuse psychiatric medications. New policies he is introducing aim to change that.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Barry

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Monday’s announcements were Health Secretary Kennedy’s first significant step to reduce the use of antidepressants.

item.title: This video was published in error.
item.description: An advance video was inadvertently published on this page earlier.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: Student News Quiz: Supreme Court, Movie Sequel, Middle Schoolers
item.description: Have you been paying attention to current events recently? See how well you can do on this week’s news quiz for students.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Engle

media:content.media:credit: Rob Carr/Getty Images

item.title: California Seeks ‘Historic’ Fine Against State Farm: Just $2 Million
item.description: Regulators say the penalty is the highest ever sought, but a cap on each violation would soften the blow for California’s largest insurer.
item.dc:creator: Rukmini Callimachi and Blacki Migliozzi

media:content.media:credit: Justin Sullivan/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Complaints have been mounting against State Farm, California’s largest homeowner’s insurer.

item.title: Mayor Mamdani Highlights Fashion Workers as He Skips Met Gala
item.description: New York’s mayor highlighted the seamstresses, tailors and other workers in the fashion industry.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher

media:content.media:credit: Madison Swart for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani in New York, last month.

item.title: Gala del Met 2025: fotos de la alfombra
item.description: Contempla los atuendos más vistosos.
item.dc:creator: The Styles Desk, Nina Westervelt and Amir Hamja

item.title: A Movie About Women Who Love Their Work? Groundbreaking.
item.description: It turns out that some women do, in fact, dream of labor, at least the kind that comes with a sense of mastery, agency and glamour.
item.dc:creator: Michelle Goldberg

item.title: S.E.C. Settles Lawsuit Against Elon Musk Over His Twitter Disclosures
item.description: The agency, which has been pulling back on lawsuits against major companies, ended a case that had accused Mr. Musk of hiding his purchases of Twitter stock. He agreed to pay $1.5 million.
item.dc:creator: Cecilia Kang

media:content.media:credit: Jason Henry for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Elon Musk arriving at federal court in San Francisco in March.

item.title: Soil at D.C. Golf Course Where East Wing Debris Was Dumped Contains Toxic Metals
item.description: The National Park Service said the dump of debris, between the fourth and ninth holes, does not exceed environmental limits. Opponents disagree.
item.dc:creator: Maxine Joselow

media:content.media:credit: Kent Nishimura for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Golfing in view of a dump site of debris and soil from the demolition of the White House’s East Wing at the East Potomac Golf Course in Washington in January.

item.title: Met Gala 2026: Rihanna Closes Out a Night of Fashion One-Upmanship
item.description: In a sea of adventurous looks, Beyoncé, Heidi Klum, Chase Infiniti and Madonna managed to stand out. The fund-raiser drew $42 million and vigorous protests against one of the event’s chairs.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Amir Hamja for The New York Times
